    Muon Orthogonalization Contracts #

    Muon is more than momentum with a different name: after the momentum buffer is updated, the parameter direction is supposed to be an orthogonalized matrix direction. The runtime optimizer therefore takes an explicit orthogonalizer backend, while this file records the proof contract that such a backend should satisfy.

    There are two useful levels:

    • ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer says the backend returns a matrix Q with QᵀQ = I.
    • ApproxMatrixOrthogonalizer eps says the Gram residual QᵀQ - I is entrywise bounded by eps.

    The theorems below connect those contracts to the executable Muon step: if the backend satisfies one of these contracts, the direction used in the parameter update is certified at the same level.

    source
    🔸 coverage
    @[reducible, inline]
    abbrev Optim.Muon.MatrixTensor (α : Type) (m n : ℕ) :
    Type

    A matrix-shaped TorchLean tensor.

    Equations
    • Optim.Muon.MatrixTensor α m n = Spec.Tensor α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))
    Instances For
      source
      🔸 coverage
      def Optim.Muon.columnGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
      MatrixTensor α n n

      The column Gram matrix QᵀQ.

      Equations
      • Optim.Muon.columnGram Q = Spec.matMulSpec (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec Q) Q
      Instances For
        source
        🔹 coverage
        def Optim.Muon.HasExactColumnGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
        Prop

        Exact column orthogonality for a matrix-shaped update direction.

        Equations
        • Optim.Muon.HasExactColumnGram Q = (Optim.Muon.columnGram Q = Spec.identityTensorSpec n)
        Instances For
          source
          📐 coverage
          theorem Optim.Muon.hasExactColumnGram_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
          HasExactColumnGram Q ↔ columnGram Q = Spec.identityTensorSpec n

          Exact column Gram means exactly QᵀQ = I.

          source
          🔹 coverage
          def Optim.Muon.ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
          Prop

          A backend exactly orthogonalizes one specified momentum buffer.

          Equations
          • Optim.Muon.ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer orthogonalizer buffer = Optim.Muon.HasExactColumnGram (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)
          Instances For
            source
            📐 coverage
            theorem Optim.Muon.exactOrthogonalizesBuffer_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
            ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer orthogonalizer buffer ↔ HasExactColumnGram (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

            One-buffer exact orthogonalization is exact column Gram for the backend output.

            source
            🔸 coverage
            def Optim.Muon.columnGramResidual {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
            MatrixTensor α n n

            Residual matrix QᵀQ - I, used for approximate orthogonalization certificates.

            Equations
            • Optim.Muon.columnGramResidual Q = Spec.Tensor.subSpec (Optim.Muon.columnGram Q) (Spec.identityTensorSpec n)
            Instances For
              source
              📐 coverage
              theorem Optim.Muon.columnGramResidual_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
              columnGramResidual Q = Spec.Tensor.subSpec (columnGram Q) (Spec.identityTensorSpec n)

              Expanded form of the column-Gram residual QᵀQ - I.

              source
              🔹 coverage
              def Optim.Muon.HasApproxColumnGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
              Prop

              Entrywise approximate column orthogonality.

              For an exact backend use HasExactColumnGram. For Newton-Schulz or CUDA implementations, this is the certificate shape we want the backend to establish or export: every entry of QᵀQ - I is bounded by eps.

              Equations
              • Optim.Muon.HasApproxColumnGram eps Q = ∀ (i j : Fin n), MathFunctions.abs (Spec.get2 (Optim.Muon.columnGramResidual Q) i j) ≤ eps
              Instances For
                source
                📐 coverage
                theorem Optim.Muon.hasApproxColumnGram_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                HasApproxColumnGram eps Q ↔ ∀ (i j : Fin n), MathFunctions.abs (Spec.get2 (columnGramResidual Q) i j) ≤ eps

                Approximate column orthogonality is exactly the entrywise residual bound.

                source
                🔹 coverage
                def Optim.Muon.ApproxOrthogonalizesBuffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                Prop

                A backend approximately orthogonalizes one specified momentum buffer.

                Equations
                • Optim.Muon.ApproxOrthogonalizesBuffer eps orthogonalizer buffer = Optim.Muon.HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                  ApproxOrthogonalizesBuffer eps orthogonalizer buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                  One-buffer approximate orthogonalization is the residual bound for the backend output.

                  source
                  🔹 coverage
                  def Optim.Muon.ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) :
                  Prop

                  An exact matrix Muon orthogonalizer maps every momentum buffer to a direction whose columns have Gram matrix I.

                  Equations
                  • Optim.Muon.ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer orthogonalizer = ∀ (buffer : Optim.Muon.MatrixTensor α m n), Optim.Muon.HasExactColumnGram (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                    📐 coverage

                    ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer orthogonalizer ↔ ∀ (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n), HasExactColumnGram (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                    A matrix exact backend returns exact column-Gram directions for every buffer.

                      Certified backend records #

                      source
                      🔶 coverage
                      structure Optim.Muon.ExactCertifiedOrthogonalizer (α : Type) [Context α] (m n : ℕ) :
                      Type

                      Unconditionally certified exact Muon backend.

                      Use this when the orthogonalizer is known to return an exact QᵀQ = I direction for every buffer of a fixed matrix shape.

                      • orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                        Executable orthogonalizer used by Muon.

                      • certified : ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer self.orthogonalizer

                        The backend returns an exactly orthogonalized direction for every buffer.

                      Instances For
                        source
                        🔶 coverage
                        structure Optim.Muon.ApproxCertifiedOrthogonalizer (α : Type) [Context α] (m n : ℕ) (eps : α) :
                        Type

                        Unconditionally certified approximate Muon backend.

                        Use this when the orthogonalizer is known to return a direction whose Gram residual is bounded by eps for every buffer of a fixed matrix shape.

                        • orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                          Executable orthogonalizer used by Muon.

                        • certified : ApproxMatrixOrthogonalizer eps self.orthogonalizer

                          The backend returns an approximately orthogonalized direction for every buffer.

                        Instances For
                          source
                          🔶 coverage
                          structure Optim.Muon.CheckedExactOrthogonalizer (α : Type) [Context α] (m n : ℕ) :
                          Type

                          Checked exact Muon backend with a per-buffer success predicate.

                          This is the practical interface for algorithms whose correctness has preconditions on the matrix being orthogonalized. The QR backend below is the first instance: its success predicate is positive executable R pivots.

                          • orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                            Executable orthogonalizer used by Muon.

                          • Success : MatrixTensor α m n → Prop

                            Backend-specific success/certification condition for an input buffer.

                          • certified (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) : self.Success buffer → ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer self.orthogonalizer buffer

                            Whenever Success buffer holds, the backend exactly orthogonalizes that buffer.

                          Instances For
                            source
                            🔶 coverage
                            structure Optim.Muon.CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer (α : Type) [Context α] (m n : ℕ) (eps : α) :
                            Type

                            Checked approximate Muon backend with a per-buffer success predicate.

                            This is the intended proof shape for Newton-Schulz/CUDA-style backends: the kernel may be fast and approximate, but the exported proof or checker must establish Success buffer, which then gives the entrywise QᵀQ - I bound.

                            • orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                              Executable orthogonalizer used by Muon.

                            • Success : MatrixTensor α m n → Prop

                              Backend-specific success/certification condition for an input buffer.

                            • certified (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) : self.Success buffer → ApproxOrthogonalizesBuffer eps self.orthogonalizer buffer

                              Whenever Success buffer holds, the backend approximately orthogonalizes that buffer.

                            Instances For

                              Newton-Schulz-shaped approximate backends #

                              source
                              🔶 coverage
                              structure Optim.Muon.NewtonSchulzCoeffs (α : Type) :
                              Type

                              Coefficients for the odd Newton-Schulz polynomial used by Muon-style orthogonalization.

                              The column-oriented shape is X ↦ aX + bX(XᵀX) + cX(XᵀX)^2, matching the QᵀQ certificate used below. TorchLean keeps the coefficients explicit so experiments and backend-specific proofs can choose the polynomial they actually use.

                              • a : α

                                Linear coefficient.

                              • b : α

                                Cubic coefficient.

                              • c : α

                                Quintic coefficient.

                              Instances For
                                source
                                🔸 coverage
                                def Optim.Muon.leftGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                MatrixTensor α m m

                                Left Gram matrix XXᵀ, useful for row-oriented rectangular Newton-Schulz updates.

                                Equations
                                • Optim.Muon.leftGram X = Spec.matMulSpec X (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec X)
                                Instances For
                                  source
                                  🔸 coverage
                                  def Optim.Muon.rightGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                  MatrixTensor α n n

                                  Right/column Gram matrix XᵀX, matching TorchLean's column-orthogonality certificate.

                                  Equations
                                  • Optim.Muon.rightGram X = Spec.matMulSpec (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec X) X
                                  Instances For

                                    One row-oriented Newton-Schulz polynomial step using XXᵀ.

                                                🔹 coverage
                                                def Optim.Muon.ResidualApproxSuccess {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                Prop

                                                Residual-check success predicate for approximate Muon backends.

                                                This is the lightest sound checker boundary: after a backend returns a direction, prove or check that the direction's Gram residual is bounded by eps.

                                                Equations
                                                • Optim.Muon.ResidualApproxSuccess eps orthogonalizer buffer = Optim.Muon.HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)
                                                Instances For
                                                  source
                                                  📐 coverage

                                                  ResidualApproxSuccess eps orthogonalizer buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                  The residual-check success predicate is exactly the approximate Gram certificate.

                                                  source
                                                  🔸 coverage
                                                  def Optim.Muon.residualCheckedApproxOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) :
                                                  CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps

                                                  Turn any orthogonalizer into a checked approximate backend by using the Gram-residual bound itself as the success predicate.

                                                  Equations

                                                    (residualCheckedApproxOrthogonalizer eps orthogonalizer).Success buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                    source
                                                    🔸 coverage
                                                    def Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzResidualCheckedOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (eps : α) :
                                                    CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps

                                                    Newton-Schulz packaged as a checked approximate backend.

                                                    The backend is the explicit Newton-Schulz tensor program, and the success predicate is the post-check that its returned direction satisfies the requested Gram-residual bound.

                                                    Equations
                                                    • Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzResidualCheckedOrthogonalizer coeffs steps eps = Optim.Muon.residualCheckedApproxOrthogonalizer eps (Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps)
                                                    Instances For

                                                      Newton-Schulz reduction lemmas #

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.leftGram_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      leftGram X = Spec.matMulSpec X (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec X)

                                                      Expanded form of the left Gram matrix.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.rightGram_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      rightGram X = Spec.matMulSpec (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec X) X

                                                      Expanded form of the right/column Gram matrix.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftStep_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      newtonSchulzLeftStep coeffs X = have G := leftGram X; have GX := Spec.matMulSpec G X; have G2X := Spec.matMulSpec G GX; ((Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec X coeffs.a).addSpec (GX.scaleSpec coeffs.b)).addSpec (G2X.scaleSpec coeffs.c)

                                                      Expanded form of one row-oriented Newton-Schulz step.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzStep_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      newtonSchulzStep coeffs X = have G := rightGram X; have XG := Spec.matMulSpec X G; have XG2 := Spec.matMulSpec XG G; ((Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec X coeffs.a).addSpec (XG.scaleSpec coeffs.b)).addSpec (XG2.scaleSpec coeffs.c)

                                                      Expanded form of one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftIter_zero {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs 0 X = X

                                                      Zero row-oriented Newton-Schulz iterations return the input.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftIter_succ {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs (steps + 1) X = newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs steps (newtonSchulzLeftStep coeffs X)

                                                      Successor row-oriented Newton-Schulz iteration.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzIter_zero {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      newtonSchulzIter coeffs 0 X = X

                                                      Zero column-oriented Newton-Schulz iterations return the input.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzIter_succ {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      newtonSchulzIter coeffs (steps + 1) X = newtonSchulzIter coeffs steps (newtonSchulzStep coeffs X)

                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer = newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs steps buffer

                                                      Applying the row-oriented Newton-Schulz orthogonalizer runs the row-oriented iterator.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer_zero_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer coeffs 0).apply buffer = buffer

                                                      Zero row-oriented Newton-Schulz steps make the orthogonalizer the identity on buffers.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer = newtonSchulzIter coeffs steps buffer

                                                      Applying the column-oriented Newton-Schulz orthogonalizer runs the column-oriented iterator.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer_zero_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0).apply buffer = buffer

                                                      Zero column-oriented Newton-Schulz steps make the orthogonalizer the identity on buffers.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.update_newtonSchulz_zero_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0 } params grads).1.buf = (MomentumSGD.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf } params grads).1.buf

                                                      With zero Newton-Schulz steps, the Muon backend leaves the fresh momentum buffer unchanged, so the stored buffer agrees with momentum SGD's stored buffer.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.update_newtonSchulz_zero_params_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0 } params grads).2 = (MomentumSGD.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf } params grads).2

                                                      With zero Newton-Schulz steps, Muon's parameter update is exactly momentum SGD's parameter update.

                                                      This is a backend sanity law: enabling the Newton-Schulz-shaped path with no iterations cannot silently change the optimizer semantics.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.init_newtonSchulz_zero_update_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (update (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0) params) params grads).1.buf = (MomentumSGD.update (MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).1.buf

                                                      Starting from initialized states, a zero-step Newton-Schulz Muon backend stores the same next buffer as momentum SGD.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.init_newtonSchulz_zero_update_params_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (update (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0) params) params grads).2 = (MomentumSGD.update (MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).2

                                                      Starting from initialized states, a zero-step Newton-Schulz Muon backend has the same parameter update as momentum SGD.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzResidualChecked_success_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (eps : α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (newtonSchulzResidualCheckedOrthogonalizer coeffs steps eps).Success buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps ((newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer)

                                                      The success predicate of the Newton-Schulz residual-checked backend is the approximate Gram certificate for the Newton-Schulz output.

                                                      source
                                                      📐 coverage
                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzResidualChecked_zero_success_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (eps : α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      (newtonSchulzResidualCheckedOrthogonalizer coeffs 0 eps).Success buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps buffer

                                                      For a zero-step Newton-Schulz backend, the residual checker is checking the raw momentum buffer.

                                                      source
                                                      🔹 coverage
                                                      def Optim.Muon.NewtonSchulzFixedPoint {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                      Prop

                                                      A buffer is a fixed point of one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step.

                                                      This is a deliberately local condition. It does not assert convergence from arbitrary inputs; it records the exact algebraic fact needed when a backend has already reached a stable direction.

                                                      Equations
                                                      • Optim.Muon.NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs buffer = (Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzStep coeffs buffer = buffer)
                                                      Instances For
                                                        source
                                                        📐 coverage
                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzFixedPoint_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                        NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs buffer ↔ newtonSchulzStep coeffs buffer = buffer

                                                        The fixed-point predicate is exactly equality with one Newton-Schulz step.

                                                        source
                                                        📐 coverage
                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzIter_fixed_of_step_fixed {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) (hfixed : NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs buffer) :
                                                        newtonSchulzIter coeffs steps buffer = buffer

                                                        If one Newton-Schulz step fixes a buffer, then any finite number of Newton-Schulz iterations fixes the same buffer.

                                                        source
                                                        📐 coverage
                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer_fixed_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) (hfixed : NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs buffer) :
                                                        (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer = buffer

                                                        A Newton-Schulz fixed point is returned unchanged by the corresponding orthogonalizer.

                                                        source
                                                        📐 coverage
                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzFixedPoint_exactOrthogonalizesBuffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram buffer) (hfixed : NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs buffer) :
                                                        ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) buffer

                                                        If a buffer already has exact column Gram and is fixed by one Newton-Schulz step, then the finite-iteration Newton-Schulz backend exactly orthogonalizes that buffer.

                                                        source
                                                        🔸 coverage
                                                        def Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzFixedPointCheckedExactOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) :
                                                        CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n

                                                        Newton-Schulz packaged as a checked exact backend for already-stable directions.

                                                        The success predicate says that the input buffer already has exact column Gram and is a fixed point of one Newton-Schulz step. Under that explicit condition, every finite Newton-Schulz iteration returns the same exact-column-orthogonal direction.

                                                        Equations
                                                        • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                                                        Instances For
                                                          source
                                                          📐 coverage
                                                          theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzFixedPointCheckedExact_success_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                          (newtonSchulzFixedPointCheckedExactOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).Success buffer ↔ HasExactColumnGram buffer ∧ NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs buffer

                                                          Success for the fixed-point Newton-Schulz exact backend is the explicit Gram/fixed predicate.

                                                          source
                                                          🔶 coverage
                                                          structure Optim.Muon.ExactCertifiedDirection {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer direction : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                          Prop

                                                          A direction is certified when it is exactly the backend output and has exact column Gram I.

                                                          • direction_eq : direction = orthogonalizer.apply buffer

                                                            The direction is the one returned by the Muon orthogonalizer.

                                                          • exact_column_gram : HasExactColumnGram direction

                                                            The returned direction has exact column Gram I.

                                                          Instances For
                                                            source
                                                            🔶 coverage
                                                            structure Optim.Muon.ApproxCertifiedDirection {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer direction : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                            Prop

                                                            A direction is approximately certified when it is exactly the backend output and its Gram residual is entrywise bounded by eps.

                                                            • direction_eq : direction = orthogonalizer.apply buffer

                                                              The direction is the one returned by the Muon orthogonalizer.

                                                            • approx_column_gram : HasApproxColumnGram eps direction

                                                              The returned direction has an entrywise Gram residual bound.

                                                            Instances For
                                                              source
                                                              🔶 coverage
                                                              structure Optim.Muon.ExactCertifiedStep {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads direction : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                              Prop

                                                              An exact certified Muon step packages the whole update fact: the backend-produced direction is exactly certified, the next state is the old state with the fresh momentum buffer installed, and the parameter update subtracts the scaled certified direction.

                                                              • direction_cert : ExactCertifiedDirection state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf direction

                                                                The update direction is exactly certified for the fresh momentum buffer.

                                                              • state_eq : (update state params grads).1 = { lr := state.lr, momentum := state.momentum, buf := OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads, orthogonalizer := state.orthogonalizer }

                                                                The next optimizer state only replaces the momentum buffer.

                                                              • params_eq : (update state params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction state.lr)

                                                                The parameter update subtracts the scaled certified direction.

                                                              Instances For
                                                                source
                                                                🔶 coverage
                                                                structure Optim.Muon.ApproxCertifiedStep {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads direction : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                Prop

                                                                An approximate certified Muon step packages the same update fact as ExactCertifiedStep, with the direction carrying an entrywise Gram-residual bound.

                                                                • direction_cert : ApproxCertifiedDirection eps state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf direction

                                                                  The update direction is approximately certified for the fresh momentum buffer.

                                                                • state_eq : (update state params grads).1 = { lr := state.lr, momentum := state.momentum, buf := OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads, orthogonalizer := state.orthogonalizer }

                                                                  The next optimizer state only replaces the momentum buffer.

                                                                • params_eq : (update state params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction state.lr)

                                                                  The parameter update subtracts the scaled certified direction.

                                                                Instances For

                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.exact_certified_direction_of_orthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))} (horth : ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer orthogonalizer) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                  ExactCertifiedDirection orthogonalizer buffer (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                  An exact orthogonalizer certifies its own output direction.

                                                                  📐 coverage

                                                                  ApproxCertifiedDirection eps orthogonalizer buffer (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                  An approximate orthogonalizer certifies its own output direction.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulz_residual_certifies_direction {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) (hresidual : ResidualApproxSuccess eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) buffer) :
                                                                  ApproxCertifiedDirection eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) buffer ((newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer)

                                                                  A residual proof for the Newton-Schulz output directly gives the approximate direction certificate for that buffer.

                                                                  ExactCertifiedStep state params grads direction

                                                                  A certified exact direction for the fresh buffer gives a certified exact Muon step.

                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.exactCertifiedStep_state_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))} {params grads direction : MatrixTensor α m n} (hstep : ExactCertifiedStep state params grads direction) :

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_direction {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer state.orthogonalizer) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedDirection state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update state params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction state.lr)

                                                                  If the Muon backend is an exact matrix orthogonalizer, then one executable update uses a certified direction. The existential direction is not merely postulated: it is the backend applied to the fresh momentum buffer stored by the update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_step {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer state.orthogonalizer) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedStep state params grads direction

                                                                  If the Muon backend is an exact matrix orthogonalizer, then one executable update has a certified step: certified direction, exact next-state equation, and exact parameter equation.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_direction_of_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : ExactCertifiedOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedDirection backend.orthogonalizer (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Same as update_has_exact_certified_direction, but consumes a certified backend record.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage

                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedStep { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads direction

                                                                  Same as update_has_exact_certified_step, but consumes a certified backend record.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_direction_of_buffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedDirection state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update state params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction state.lr)

                                                                  Local-buffer version of the exact Muon certificate. This is useful for concrete orthogonalizers such as QR, where exactness may require a success condition on the particular fresh momentum buffer.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_step_of_buffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedStep state params grads direction

                                                                  Local-buffer version of the exact certified Muon step theorem.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_direction_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedDirection backend.orthogonalizer (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Checked-backend version of the exact Muon certificate. The success predicate is checked on the fresh momentum buffer actually used by the step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_step_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedStep { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads direction

                                                                  Checked-backend version of the exact certified Muon step. The success predicate is checked on the fresh momentum buffer actually used by the step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_direction_has_exact_column_gram_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  HasExactColumnGram (backend.orthogonalizer.apply (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  Checked exact backends give the concrete QᵀQ = I fact for the direction used by this update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_exact_certified_direction_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedDirection backend.orthogonalizer (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Initialized checked exact backend theorem: after Muon is initialized with a checked exact backend, success on the fresh momentum buffer certifies the direction used by the first update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_exact_certified_step_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedStep (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads direction

                                                                  Initialized checked exact backend theorem: after Muon is initialized with a checked exact backend, success on the fresh momentum buffer gives a certified first step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_direction_has_exact_column_gram_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (backend : CheckedExactOrthogonalizer α m n) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  HasExactColumnGram (backend.orthogonalizer.apply (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  Initialized checked exact backend direction theorem: after initialization, backend success gives QᵀQ = I for the actual direction used by the first Muon update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_direction_newtonSchulz_fixed_checked {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : (newtonSchulzFixedPointCheckedExactOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedDirection (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  A fixed-point checked Newton-Schulz backend gives an exact certified direction for one Muon update.

                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ExactCertifiedStep { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads direction

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_direction {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ApproxMatrixOrthogonalizer eps state.orthogonalizer) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update state params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction state.lr)

                                                                  If the Muon backend is an approximate matrix orthogonalizer, then one executable update uses a direction whose Gram residual has the same entrywise bound.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_step {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ApproxMatrixOrthogonalizer eps state.orthogonalizer) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedStep eps state params grads direction

                                                                  If the Muon backend is an approximate matrix orthogonalizer, then one executable update has a certified approximate step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_direction_of_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : ApproxCertifiedOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps backend.orthogonalizer (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Same as update_has_approx_certified_direction, but consumes a certified backend record.

                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedStep eps { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads direction

                                                                  Same as update_has_approx_certified_step, but consumes a certified backend record.

                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_direction_of_buffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ApproxOrthogonalizesBuffer eps state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps state.orthogonalizer (update state params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update state params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction state.lr)

                                                                  Local-buffer version of the approximate Muon certificate. This lets a backend certify only the fresh momentum buffer used by the current step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage

                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedStep eps state params grads direction

                                                                  Local-buffer version of the approximate certified Muon step theorem.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_direction_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps backend.orthogonalizer (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Checked-backend version of the approximate Muon certificate. This is the main theorem a Newton-Schulz/CUDA backend should target: once the checker establishes the backend success predicate on the fresh momentum buffer, the Muon update is known to use an approximately column-orthogonal direction.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_step_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedStep eps { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads direction

                                                                  Checked-backend version of the approximate certified Muon step. This is the step-level theorem a residual-checked Newton-Schulz/CUDA backend should target.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_direction_has_approx_column_gram_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  HasApproxColumnGram eps (backend.orthogonalizer.apply (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := backend.orthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  Checked approximate backends give the concrete residual bound for the direction used by this update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_approx_certified_direction_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps backend.orthogonalizer (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Initialized checked approximate backend theorem: after Muon is initialized with a checked approximate backend, success on the fresh momentum buffer certifies the direction used by the first update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_approx_certified_step_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedStep eps (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads direction

                                                                  Initialized checked approximate backend theorem: after Muon is initialized with a checked approximate backend, success on the fresh momentum buffer gives a certified first step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_direction_has_approx_column_gram_of_checked_backend {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (backend : CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hsuccess : backend.Success (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  HasApproxColumnGram eps (backend.orthogonalizer.apply (update (init lr momentum backend.orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  Initialized checked approximate backend direction theorem: after initialization, backend success gives the residual bound for the actual direction used by the first Muon update.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_direction_newtonSchulz_checked {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hresidual : ResidualApproxSuccess eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Checked Newton-Schulz Muon theorem: if the Newton-Schulz output for the fresh momentum buffer passes the residual check, then the executable Muon update uses an approximately column-orthogonal direction.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_approx_certified_step_newtonSchulz_checked {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hresidual : ResidualApproxSuccess eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedStep eps { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads direction

                                                                  Checked Newton-Schulz Muon step theorem: if the Newton-Schulz output for the fresh momentum buffer passes the residual check, then the executable Muon update has a certified approximate step.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_newtonSchulz_direction_has_approx_column_gram_checked {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hresidual : ResidualApproxSuccess eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  HasApproxColumnGram eps ((newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  Checked Newton-Schulz direction theorem: if the fresh momentum buffer passes the residual check, then the actual direction used by the Muon update has the requested Gram-residual bound.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_approx_certified_direction_newtonSchulz_checked {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} {eps : α} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (hresidual : ResidualApproxSuccess eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                  ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor α m n), ApproxCertifiedDirection eps (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) (update (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) params) params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) params) params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                  Initialized checked Newton-Schulz theorem: if the first fresh momentum buffer passes the residual check, then the first initialized Muon update uses an approximately certified direction.

                                                                  source
                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                  theorem Optim.Muon.update_direction_has_exact_column_gram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (state : State α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) (horth : ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer state.orthogonalizer) :
                                                                  HasExactColumnGram (state.orthogonalizer.apply (update state params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  The exact certificate gives the concrete QᵀQ = I fact for the direction used by the update.
