                                                                  HasApproxColumnGram eps (state.orthogonalizer.apply (update state params grads).1.buf)

                                                                  The approximate certificate gives the concrete residual bound for the direction used by the update.

                                                                  QR as a concrete exact orthogonalizer over ℝ #

                                                                  source
                                                                  🔸 coverage
                                                                  noncomputable def Optim.Muon.qrOrthogonalizer {m n : ℕ} :
                                                                  Orthogonalizer ℝ (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                                                                  QR/Gram-Schmidt orthogonalizer: return the Q factor of the fresh matrix buffer.

                                                                  Equations
                                                                  • Optim.Muon.qrOrthogonalizer = { apply := fun (buffer : Spec.Tensor ℝ (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) => Spec.qrQSpec buffer }
                                                                  Instances For
                                                                    source
                                                                    🔹 coverage
                                                                    def Optim.Muon.HasPositiveQRPivots {m n : ℕ} (buffer : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) :
                                                                    Prop

                                                                    The success condition for TorchLean's executable QR orthogonalizer.

                                                                    Equations
                                                                    • Optim.Muon.HasPositiveQRPivots buffer = ∀ (j : Fin n), 0 < Spec.get2 (Spec.qrRSpec buffer) j j
                                                                    Instances For
                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.get2_identityTensorSpec_real {n : ℕ} (i j : Fin n) :
                                                                      Spec.get2 (Spec.identityTensorSpec n) i j = if i = j then 1 else 0
                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.get2_addSpec_real {m n : ℕ} (A B : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (i : Fin m) (j : Fin n) :
                                                                      Spec.get2 (Spec.Tensor.addSpec A B) i j = Spec.get2 A i j + Spec.get2 B i j

                                                                      Entry rule for matrix-shaped tensor addition over ℝ.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.get2_scaleSpec_real {m n : ℕ} (A : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (c : ℝ) (i : Fin m) (j : Fin n) :
                                                                      Spec.get2 (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec A c) i j = Spec.get2 A i j * c

                                                                      Entry rule for matrix-shaped tensor scaling over ℝ.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.get2_subSpec_real {m n : ℕ} (A B : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (i : Fin m) (j : Fin n) :
                                                                      Spec.get2 (Spec.Tensor.subSpec A B) i j = Spec.get2 A i j - Spec.get2 B i j

                                                                      Entry rule for matrix-shaped tensor subtraction over ℝ.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.matMul_right_identity_real {m n : ℕ} (A : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) :
                                                                      Spec.matMulSpec A (Spec.identityTensorSpec n) = A

                                                                      Right multiplication by the identity matrix leaves a real matrix unchanged.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.add_scaled_three_eq_scale_sum {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (a b c : ℝ) :
                                                                      ((Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q a).addSpec (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q b)).addSpec (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q c) = Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q (a + b + c)

                                                                      Three scaled copies of the same real matrix combine into one scaled copy using the sum of the coefficients.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.add_scaled_three_eq_self_of_coeff_sum_one {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (a b c : ℝ) (hsum : a + b + c = 1) :
                                                                      ((Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q a).addSpec (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q b)).addSpec (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q c) = Q

                                                                      If three scaled copies of a matrix are added and the coefficients sum to one, the result is the original matrix.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.scale_hasExactColumnGram_of_square_eq_one {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (k : ℝ) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram Q) (hk : k * k = 1) :
                                                                      HasExactColumnGram (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q k)

                                                                      Scaling an exact-column-orthogonal real matrix by a scalar whose square is one preserves exact column Gram.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.scale_hasApproxColumnGram_of_exact_column_gram_of_square_error {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (k eps : ℝ) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram Q) (herr : MathFunctions.abs (k * k - 1) ≤ eps) (heps : 0 ≤ eps) :
                                                                      HasApproxColumnGram eps (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q k)

                                                                      Scaling an exact-column-orthogonal real matrix gives an approximate Gram certificate whenever |k^2 - 1| is bounded by the requested tolerance.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzStep_eq_scale_sum_of_exact_column_gram {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram Q) :
                                                                      newtonSchulzStep coeffs Q = Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec Q (coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c)

                                                                      If QᵀQ = I, then one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step returns (a + b + c) Q.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzFixedPoint_of_exact_column_gram_of_coeff_sum_one {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram Q) (hsum : coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c = 1) :
                                                                      NewtonSchulzFixedPoint coeffs Q

                                                                      For real coefficients whose sum is one, an exact-column-orthogonal matrix is a fixed point of one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzStep_hasExactColumnGram_of_exact_column_gram_of_sum_square_one {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram Q) (hsquare : (coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c) * (coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c) = 1) :
                                                                      HasExactColumnGram (newtonSchulzStep coeffs Q)

                                                                      If QᵀQ = I and (a + b + c)^2 = 1, then one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step still has exact column Gram.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzStep_hasApproxColumnGram_of_exact_column_gram_of_sum_square_error {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (Q : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (eps : ℝ) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram Q) (herr : MathFunctions.abs ((coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c) * (coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c) - 1) ≤ eps) (heps : 0 ≤ eps) :
                                                                      HasApproxColumnGram eps (newtonSchulzStep coeffs Q)

                                                                      If QᵀQ = I and |(a + b + c)^2 - 1| ≤ eps, then one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step has entrywise Gram residual bounded by eps.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzFixedPointCheckedExact_success_of_coeff_sum_one {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram buffer) (hsum : coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c = 1) :
                                                                      (newtonSchulzFixedPointCheckedExactOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).Success buffer

                                                                      If the Newton-Schulz coefficients sum to one, exact column Gram is enough to satisfy the exact fixed-point backend's success predicate.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_step_newtonSchulz_exact_gram_checked {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : ℝ) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf) (hsum : coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c = 1) :
                                                                      ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor ℝ m n), ExactCertifiedStep { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads direction

                                                                      For real coefficients with a + b + c = 1, exact column Gram of the fresh momentum buffer is enough to certify a Newton-Schulz Muon update exactly.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.update_newtonSchulz_exact_gram_direction_has_exact_column_gram_checked {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : ℝ) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf) (hsum : coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c = 1) :
                                                                      HasExactColumnGram ((newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps } params grads).1.buf)

                                                                      For real coefficients with a + b + c = 1, exact column Gram of the fresh momentum buffer gives QᵀQ = I for the actual Newton-Schulz update direction.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_exact_certified_step_newtonSchulz_exact_gram_checked {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs ℝ) (steps : ℕ) (lr momentum : ℝ) (params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hgram : HasExactColumnGram (update (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) params) params grads).1.buf) (hsum : coeffs.a + coeffs.b + coeffs.c = 1) :
                                                                      ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor ℝ m n), ExactCertifiedStep (init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps) params) params grads direction

                                                                      Initialized version: exact column Gram of the first fresh momentum buffer and a + b + c = 1 certify the first Newton-Schulz Muon step exactly.

                                                                      source
                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                      theorem Optim.Muon.qrOrthogonalizer_exact_of_positive_pivots {m n : ℕ} (buffer : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots buffer) :
                                                                      ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer qrOrthogonalizer buffer

                                                                      The QR orthogonalizer satisfies the exact Muon direction contract whenever the executable QR pivots of the input buffer are positive.

                                                                      source
                                                                      🔸 coverage
                                                                      noncomputable def Optim.Muon.qrCheckedExactOrthogonalizer {m n : ℕ} :
                                                                      CheckedExactOrthogonalizer ℝ m n

                                                                      QR packaged as a checked exact Muon backend.

                                                                      Equations
                                                                      • Optim.Muon.qrCheckedExactOrthogonalizer = { orthogonalizer := Optim.Muon.qrOrthogonalizer, Success := Optim.Muon.HasPositiveQRPivots, certified := ⋯ }
                                                                      Instances For
                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.qrCheckedExactOrthogonalizer_success_iff {m n : ℕ} (buffer : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) :
                                                                        qrCheckedExactOrthogonalizer.Success buffer ↔ HasPositiveQRPivots buffer

                                                                        The QR checked backend succeeds exactly when the executable QR pivots are positive.

                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_direction_qr {m n : ℕ} (lr momentum : ℝ) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                        ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor ℝ m n), ExactCertifiedDirection qrOrthogonalizer (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                        Concrete QR-backed Muon theorem: if the fresh momentum buffer has positive QR pivots, the executable Muon update uses a direction with exact column Gram I.

                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.update_has_exact_certified_step_qr {m n : ℕ} (lr momentum : ℝ) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                        ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor ℝ m n), ExactCertifiedStep { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads direction

                                                                        Concrete QR-backed Muon step theorem: if the fresh momentum buffer has positive QR pivots, the executable Muon update has a certified exact step.

                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.update_qr_direction_has_exact_column_gram {m n : ℕ} (lr momentum : ℝ) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                        HasExactColumnGram (qrOrthogonalizer.apply (update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := qrOrthogonalizer } params grads).1.buf)

                                                                        Concrete QR-backed direction theorem: if the fresh momentum buffer has positive QR pivots, the actual direction used by the Muon update has column Gram I.

                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_exact_certified_direction_qr {m n : ℕ} (lr momentum : ℝ) (params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots (update (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                        ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor ℝ m n), ExactCertifiedDirection qrOrthogonalizer (update (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf direction ∧ (update (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads).2 = Spec.Tensor.subSpec params (Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec direction lr)

                                                                        Initialized QR-backed Muon theorem: if the first fresh momentum buffer has positive QR pivots, the first initialized Muon update uses a direction with exact column Gram I.

                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.init_has_exact_certified_step_qr {m n : ℕ} (lr momentum : ℝ) (params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots (update (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                        ∃ (direction : MatrixTensor ℝ m n), ExactCertifiedStep (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads direction

                                                                        Initialized QR-backed Muon step theorem: if the first fresh momentum buffer has positive QR pivots, the first initialized Muon update has a certified exact step.

                                                                        source
                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                        theorem Optim.Muon.init_qr_direction_has_exact_column_gram {m n : ℕ} (lr momentum : ℝ) (params grads : MatrixTensor ℝ m n) (hpivots : HasPositiveQRPivots (update (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf) :
                                                                        HasExactColumnGram (qrOrthogonalizer.apply (update (init lr momentum qrOrthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf)

                                                                        Initialized QR-backed direction theorem: if the first fresh momentum buffer has positive QR pivots, the first initialized Muon update direction has column Gram I.

                                                                                abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.initSpec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                State α s

                                                                                The proof layer Muon initialization equation.

                                                                                Equations

                                                                                  source

                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params = initSpec lr momentum orthogonalizer params

                                                                                  The executable Muon initializer follows initSpec.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_lr_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params).lr = lr

                                                                                  Muon initialization stores the requested learning rate.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_momentum_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params).momentum = momentum

                                                                                  Muon initialization stores the requested momentum coefficient.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_buffer_eq_zero {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params).buf = Spec.fill 0 s

                                                                                  Muon initialization starts from the all-zero momentum buffer.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_orthogonalizer_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params).orthogonalizer = orthogonalizer

                                                                                  Muon initialization stores exactly the orthogonalizer backend supplied by the caller.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_update_buffer_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf = Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf (Spec.fill 0 s) momentum grads

                                                                                  After initialization, Muon's first step stores the momentum update from the zero buffer.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  📐 coverage
                                                                                  theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_update_params_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params) params grads).2 = have newBuf := Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf (Spec.fill 0 s) momentum grads; params.subSpec ((orthogonalizer.apply newBuf).scaleSpec lr)

                                                                                  After initialization, Muon's first parameter update applies the supplied backend to the fresh momentum buffer computed from the zero buffer.

                                                                                  source
                                                                                  🔸 coverage
                                                                                  @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                  abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.updateSpec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                  State α s × Spec.Tensor α s

                                                                                  The proof layer Muon update equation.

                                                                                  Instances For
                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    Optim.Muon.update state params grads = updateSpec state params grads

                                                                                    The executable Muon update follows updateSpec.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_state_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).1 = { lr := state.lr, momentum := state.momentum, buf := Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads, orthogonalizer := state.orthogonalizer }

                                                                                    Muon's next state is the old state with only the momentum buffer replaced.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_update_state_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params) params grads).1 = { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf (Spec.fill 0 s) momentum grads, orthogonalizer := orthogonalizer }

                                                                                    After initialization, Muon's first next state has the requested scalars/backend and fresh buffer.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_buffer_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).1.buf = Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads

                                                                                    Muon's next buffer is the momentum update momentum * buf + grad.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_lr_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).1.lr = state.lr

                                                                                    A Muon update preserves the learning rate stored in the optimizer state.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_momentum_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).1.momentum = state.momentum

                                                                                    A Muon update preserves the momentum coefficient stored in the optimizer state.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_orthogonalizer_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).1.orthogonalizer = state.orthogonalizer

                                                                                    A Muon update preserves the configured orthogonalizer backend.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_params_eq_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).2 = have newBuf := Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads; params.subSpec ((state.orthogonalizer.apply newBuf).scaleSpec state.lr)

                                                                                    Muon's parameter update uses the orthogonalizer applied to the fresh momentum buffer.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).1.buf = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update { lr := state.lr, momentum := state.momentum, buf := state.buf } params grads).1.buf

                                                                                    For any orthogonalizer backend, Muon's stored momentum buffer evolves exactly like momentum SGD. The backend changes the parameter direction, not the buffer recurrence.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_update_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update (Optim.MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).1.buf

                                                                                    Starting from initialized states, Muon's first stored momentum buffer agrees with momentum SGD for any orthogonalizer backend.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_params_eq_momentumSGD_of_apply_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (state : State α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) (happly : state.orthogonalizer.apply (Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads) = Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf state.buf state.momentum grads) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update state params grads).2 = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update { lr := state.lr, momentum := state.momentum, buf := state.buf } params grads).2

                                                                                    If a Muon backend returns the fresh momentum buffer unchanged on this step, then the parameter update agrees with momentum SGD for this step.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_update_params_eq_momentumSGD_of_apply_eq {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (orthogonalizer : Orthogonalizer α s) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) (happly : orthogonalizer.apply (Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf (Spec.fill 0 s) momentum grads) = Optim.OptimizerUtils.updateMomentumBuf (Spec.fill 0 s) momentum grads) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum orthogonalizer params) params grads).2 = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update (Optim.MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).2

                                                                                    Initialized version of update_params_eq_momentumSGD_of_apply_eq.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_identity_params_eq_momentumSGD_spec {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := identityOrthogonalizer } params grads).2 = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf } params grads).2

                                                                                    With the identity orthogonalizer, Muon has the same parameter update as momentum SGD.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_identity_update_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum identityOrthogonalizer params) params grads).1.buf = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update (Optim.MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).1.buf

                                                                                    Starting from initialized states, identity-backend Muon stores the same next buffer as momentum SGD.

                                                                                    source
                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_identity_update_params_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] [DecidableRel fun (x1 x2 : α) => x1 > x2] {s : Spec.Shape} (lr momentum : α) (params grads : Spec.Tensor α s) :
                                                                                    (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum identityOrthogonalizer params) params grads).2 = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update (Optim.MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).2

                                                                                    Starting from initialized states, identity-backend Muon has the same parameter update as momentum SGD.

                                                                                      MatrixTensor α n n

                                                                                      Residual matrix QᵀQ - I for a matrix update direction.

                                                                                      Instances For
                                                                                        source
                                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                                        theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.columnGramResidual_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                        columnGramResidual Q = Spec.Tensor.subSpec (columnGram Q) (Spec.identityTensorSpec n)

                                                                                        Expanded form of the column-Gram residual QᵀQ - I.

                                                                                        source
                                                                                        🔹 coverage
                                                                                        @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                        abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.HasExactColumnGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                        Prop

                                                                                        Exact Muon direction contract: the direction has column Gram matrix I.

                                                                                        Equations
                                                                                        • TorchLean.optim.muon.HasExactColumnGram Q = Optim.Muon.HasExactColumnGram Q
                                                                                        Instances For
                                                                                          source
                                                                                          📐 coverage
                                                                                          theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.hasExactColumnGram_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                          HasExactColumnGram Q ↔ columnGram Q = Spec.identityTensorSpec n

                                                                                          Exact column Gram means exactly QᵀQ = I.

                                                                                          source
                                                                                          🔹 coverage
                                                                                          @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                          abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.HasApproxColumnGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                          Prop

                                                                                          Approximate Muon direction contract: QᵀQ - I is entrywise bounded.

                                                                                          Equations
                                                                                          • TorchLean.optim.muon.HasApproxColumnGram eps Q = Optim.Muon.HasApproxColumnGram eps Q
                                                                                          Instances For
                                                                                            source
                                                                                            📐 coverage
                                                                                            theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.hasApproxColumnGram_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (Q : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                            HasApproxColumnGram eps Q ↔ ∀ (i j : Fin n), MathFunctions.abs (Spec.get2 (columnGramResidual Q) i j) ≤ eps

                                                                                            Approximate column orthogonality is exactly the entrywise residual bound.

                                                                                            source
                                                                                            🔹 coverage
                                                                                            @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                            abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) :
                                                                                            Prop

                                                                                            Exact orthogonalizer contract for every matrix buffer of a fixed shape.

                                                                                            Equations
                                                                                            • TorchLean.optim.muon.ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer orthogonalizer = Optim.Muon.ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer orthogonalizer

                                                                                              abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                              Prop

                                                                                              Exact orthogonalization contract for one concrete momentum buffer.

                                                                                              Equations

                                                                                                source

                                                                                                theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.exactOrthogonalizesBuffer_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                ExactOrthogonalizesBuffer orthogonalizer buffer ↔ HasExactColumnGram (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                                                One-buffer exact orthogonalization is exact column Gram for the backend output.

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                🔹 coverage

                                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.approxOrthogonalizesBuffer_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (orthogonalizer : Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                    ApproxOrthogonalizesBuffer eps orthogonalizer buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                                                    One-buffer approximate orthogonalization is the residual bound for the backend output.

                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.exactMatrixOrthogonalizer_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (orthogonalizer : Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) :
                                                                                                    ExactMatrixOrthogonalizer orthogonalizer ↔ ∀ (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n), HasExactColumnGram (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                                                    A matrix exact backend returns exact column-Gram directions for every buffer.

                                                                                                    source

                                                                                                    ApproxMatrixOrthogonalizer eps orthogonalizer ↔ ∀ (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n), HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                                                              abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.leftGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                              MatrixTensor α m m

                                                                                                              Left Gram matrix XXᵀ, used by the Newton-Schulz backend.

                                                                                                                theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.leftGram_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                leftGram X = Spec.matMulSpec X (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec X)

                                                                                                                Expanded form of the left Gram matrix.

                                                                                                                source

                                                                                                                abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.rightGram {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                MatrixTensor α n n

                                                                                                                Right/column Gram matrix XᵀX, matching the Muon column-Gram certificate.

                                                                                                                Equations

                                                                                                                  rightGram X = Spec.matMulSpec (Spec.Tensor.matrixTransposeSpec X) X

                                                                                                                  Expanded form of the right/column Gram matrix.

                                                                                                                  🔸 coverage
                                                                                                                  @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                                                  abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftStep {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                  MatrixTensor α m n

                                                                                                                  One row-oriented Newton-Schulz polynomial step using XXᵀ.

                                                                                                                  Equations
                                                                                                                  • TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftStep coeffs X = Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftStep coeffs X
                                                                                                                  Instances For
                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                    📐 coverage
                                                                                                                    theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftStep_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                    newtonSchulzLeftStep coeffs X = have G := leftGram X; have GX := Spec.matMulSpec G X; have G2X := Spec.matMulSpec G GX; ((Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec X coeffs.a).addSpec (GX.scaleSpec coeffs.b)).addSpec (G2X.scaleSpec coeffs.c)

                                                                                                                    Expanded form of one row-oriented Newton-Schulz step.

                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                    🔸 coverage
                                                                                                                    @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                                                    abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzStep {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                    MatrixTensor α m n

                                                                                                                    One column-oriented Newton-Schulz polynomial step using XᵀX.

                                                                                                                    Equations
                                                                                                                    • TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzStep coeffs X = Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzStep coeffs X
                                                                                                                    Instances For
                                                                                                                      source
                                                                                                                      📐 coverage
                                                                                                                      theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzStep_eq {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                      newtonSchulzStep coeffs X = have G := rightGram X; have XG := Spec.matMulSpec X G; have XG2 := Spec.matMulSpec XG G; ((Spec.Tensor.scaleSpec X coeffs.a).addSpec (XG.scaleSpec coeffs.b)).addSpec (XG2.scaleSpec coeffs.c)

                                                                                                                      Expanded form of one column-oriented Newton-Schulz step.

                                                                                                                      source

                                                                                                                      MatrixTensor α m n

                                                                                                                      Fixed-count row-oriented Newton-Schulz polynomial iteration.

                                                                                                                      Equations
                                                                                                                      • TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs steps X = Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs steps X
                                                                                                                      Instances For
                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                                                                        theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftIter_zero {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                        newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs 0 X = X

                                                                                                                        Zero row-oriented Newton-Schulz iterations return the input.

                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                        📐 coverage
                                                                                                                        theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftIter_succ {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                        newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs (steps + 1) X = newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs steps (newtonSchulzLeftStep coeffs X)

                                                                                                                        Successor row-oriented Newton-Schulz iteration.

                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                        🔸 coverage
                                                                                                                        @[reducible, inline]

                                                                                                                          theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzIter_zero {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                          newtonSchulzIter coeffs 0 X = X

                                                                                                                          📐 coverage
                                                                                                                          theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzIter_succ {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (X : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                          newtonSchulzIter coeffs (steps + 1) X = newtonSchulzIter coeffs steps (newtonSchulzStep coeffs X)

                                                                                                                          abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) :
                                                                                                                          Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                                                                                                                          Row-oriented Newton-Schulz-shaped orthogonalizer backend.

                                                                                                                          Equations
                                                                                                                          • TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer coeffs steps = Optim.Muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer coeffs steps
                                                                                                                          Instances For
                                                                                                                            source
                                                                                                                            📐 coverage
                                                                                                                            theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                            (newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer = newtonSchulzLeftIter coeffs steps buffer

                                                                                                                            Applying the row-oriented Newton-Schulz orthogonalizer runs the row-oriented iterator.

                                                                                                                            source
                                                                                                                            📐 coverage
                                                                                                                            theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer_zero_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                            (newtonSchulzLeftOrthogonalizer coeffs 0).apply buffer = buffer

                                                                                                                            Zero row-oriented Newton-Schulz steps make the orthogonalizer the identity on buffers.

                                                                                                                            source
                                                                                                                            🔸 coverage
                                                                                                                            @[reducible, inline]
                                                                                                                            abbrev TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) :
                                                                                                                            Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))

                                                                                                                            Column-oriented Newton-Schulz-shaped orthogonalizer backend.

                                                                                                                            Equations

                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                              📐 coverage
                                                                                                                              theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (steps : ℕ) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                              (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs steps).apply buffer = newtonSchulzIter coeffs steps buffer

                                                                                                                              Applying the column-oriented Newton-Schulz orthogonalizer runs the column-oriented iterator.

                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                              📐 coverage
                                                                                                                              theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer_zero_apply {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                              (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0).apply buffer = buffer

                                                                                                                              Zero column-oriented Newton-Schulz steps make the orthogonalizer the identity on buffers.

                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                              📐 coverage
                                                                                                                              theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_newtonSchulz_zero_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                              (Optim.Muon.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0 } params grads).1.buf = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf } params grads).1.buf

                                                                                                                              With zero Newton-Schulz steps, the Muon backend stores the same next buffer as momentum SGD.

                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                              📐 coverage
                                                                                                                              theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.update_newtonSchulz_zero_params_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (buf params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                              (Optim.Muon.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf, orthogonalizer := newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0 } params grads).2 = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update { lr := lr, momentum := momentum, buf := buf } params grads).2

                                                                                                                              With zero Newton-Schulz steps, the Muon backend gives the same parameter update as momentum SGD.

                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                              📐 coverage
                                                                                                                              theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_newtonSchulz_zero_update_buffer_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                              (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0) params) params grads).1.buf = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update (Optim.MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).1.buf

                                                                                                                              Starting from initialized states, a zero-step Newton-Schulz Muon backend stores the same next buffer as momentum SGD.

                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                              📐 coverage
                                                                                                                              theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.init_newtonSchulz_zero_update_params_eq_momentumSGD {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (coeffs : NewtonSchulzCoeffs α) (lr momentum : α) (params grads : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                              (Optim.Muon.update (Optim.Muon.init lr momentum (newtonSchulzOrthogonalizer coeffs 0) params) params grads).2 = (Optim.MomentumSGD.update (Optim.MomentumSGD.init lr momentum params) params grads).2

                                                                                                                              Starting from initialized states, a zero-step Newton-Schulz Muon backend has the same parameter update as momentum SGD.

                                                                                                                              source

                                                                                                                              Prop

                                                                                                                              Residual-check success predicate for approximate Muon backends.

                                                                                                                                theorem TorchLean.optim.muon.residualApproxSuccess_iff {α : Type} [Context α] {m n : ℕ} (eps : α) (orthogonalizer : Optim.Muon.Orthogonalizer α (Spec.Shape.dim m (Spec.Shape.dim n Spec.Shape.scalar))) (buffer : MatrixTensor α m n) :
                                                                                                                                ResidualApproxSuccess eps orthogonalizer buffer ↔ HasApproxColumnGram eps (orthogonalizer.apply buffer)

                                                                                                                                The residual-check success predicate is exactly the approximate Gram certificate.

                                                                                                                                @[reducible, inline]

                                                                                                                                CheckedApproxOrthogonalizer α m n eps

                                                                                                                                Package any orthogonalizer as a residual-checked approximate backend.
